one_liner: Neo4j is a graph database platform accessed via Bolt drivers (Java/Python/JS/etc.) or an HTTP API, authenticated with username/password (or Kerberos/bearer/custom), and available self-serve as a free-forever AuraDB cloud instance.

auth_methods.notes: Default scheme: username/password, either via driver helper (neo4j.auth.basic / GraphDatabase.driver(auth=(user,pass))) or HTTP Authorization: Basic base64(user:pass).
Bearer authentication scheme using base64-encoded token from an SSO/IdP integration (Enterprise/Aura SSO feature).
Kerberos ticket-based auth (requires Kerberos Add-on) and custom auth schemes/plugins for server-side pluggable auth.

self_serve_notes: AuraDB Free tier is self-serve, free forever, no credit card required, sign up at Neo4j Aura console; supports Cypher queries via standard drivers. Management/admin API for provisioning is more geared to paid tiers.

api_surface.notes: Primary interface is the Bolt protocol accessed through official language drivers (Java, Python, JS, .NET, Go) for running Cypher queries; an HTTP API also exists (older/legacy docs reference it, e.g. v4.2, v3.4) allowing REST-style query execution with Basic auth. Separate Aura Management REST API exists for cloud instance lifecycle (provisioning, backups, pausing) mainly for paid/automated workflows.

main_blocker: None significant; core Cypher query capability is fully self-serve via drivers/HTTP API with basic auth, and official + Labs MCP servers already exist. Aura management API automation may require paid tier for full lifecycle control.

evidence.supports: Lists supported auth schemes: basic, bearer, kerberos, custom, none.
Shows basic (username/password), kerberos, and bearer authentication code examples for Python driver.
Documents HTTP API using Basic auth via Authorization header.
AuraDB Free is self-serve, free forever, no credit card needed.
Details on AuraDB Free tier limits and self-serve sign-up.
Server-side auth configuration including LDAP, OIDC, custom plugins.

flags: Existing MCP server details (PyPI package name, official vs Labs) not verified from primary docs link, only from search summary.
HTTP API docs referenced are for older versions (4.2, 3.4); current HTTP API auth details not directly confirmed for latest version.
Aura Management API scope/self-serve limits for free tier not fully detailed in evidence.
